tool.name: check_scan_registry
tool.description: Check whether a target has already been scanned in this run to avoid duplicate work.
tool.details: Call this BEFORE running any scan (port scan, directory fuzz, vulnerability scan, etc.) to check if another agent has already covered this target. If already_scanned is true, skip the scan and move on to a different surface. This prevents duplicate cost from multiple agents re-scanning the same endpoint.

parameter.name: target
parameter.description: The target to check (URL, host:port, IP address, path, etc.)

returns.description: Response containing: - already_scanned: True if this target was already scanned - details: Scan metadata if already scanned - message: Human-readable status

tool.examples:
# Before running a port scan, check if already done
example.com:80
# Before running a directory fuzz
https://example.com/api/v1

tool.name: register_scan_target
tool.description: Register a target as scanned so other agents skip it and avoid duplicate work.
tool.details: Call this BEFORE (or immediately after) starting a scan so sibling and child agents know this surface is already covered. Use a descriptive scan_type label so the registry is informative.

parameter.name: target
parameter.description: The target being scanned (URL, host:port, IP address, path, etc.)

parameter.name: scan_type
parameter.description: Short label describing the type of scan: port_scan, directory_fuzz, sqli, xss, ssrf, lfi, xxe, auth_test, etc. (default: general)

returns.description: Response containing: - registered: True if newly registered, False if already existed - message: Human-readable status

tool.examples:
# Register before running a port scan
example.com port_scan
# Register a specific endpoint for SQL injection testing
https://example.com/api/v1/login sqli
